name: Notre-Dame de Paris: Journey Back In Time

reviews.review_description:
The intro starts promisingly, albeit being warned that parts of the scenery are not included in the game. Once the game starts, I am transported
 to an area of Notre Dame but looking at ground level, seeing along the floor and for the first 20 seconds or so, my head is locked on that scene. If I turn my head, that scene moves with me (def not VR), the controllers are non-functional. Soon after that frustrating lock-out, the view pops up to normal eye level and you have about 10 seconds to look around before you are transported to another place. I would pay decent money for a fully functional offering but this one is non-functional.

It's a free tour app. Yes, the length is short and it's a slight promotion for an Assassin's Creed game, but the level to detail is stunning. Ignore the one star reviews because this is just a short, free app. It works perfectly as it's intended to and it's a pretty cool tour.
There’s not much to this, but I guess for free it’s worth it
Unusable. Needs work. Disorienting. Where you look is not where the camera goes. Too bad.

Thank you Ubisoft for doing this, not a game but a fun little exploratory app inside the AC: Unity game engine.  I know there was a lot of factors that went into to this location specifically but think Ubisoft should do One location per game entry.
ie: Tower of London from Syndicate, Pyramids in Origins, etc.
Not much to see.  Not interactive.
PERFECTION PERFECT if you never have been before watch this and you will have been

It's just a short tour of Notre Dame with passing moments of standing in pre-selected areas inside and outside the cathedral.  More like being in Assassin's Creed Unity sets than true realism (which I think was possibly the intent, due to the intro connections to Unity). There's also a hot air balloon part that's pretty cool.

It seems like maybe it's a work in progress, or at least I hope so.  My play area is very small and it seems like if you have the room to move in your own space you could wander further in the app space to look over edges or climb some steps, yet there's no locomotion options.  That is a bit frustrating, so I hope they will at least add this feature, as well as some object resolution improvements.  Otherwise, it's a pleasant little retreat to soak in the beauty and atmosphere of the cathedral.
You just stand and watch, but it's all very crisp.

Low quality textures, even by raising the resolution to 180% with this optimizer the textures are still of low quality: The stained glass windows remain of low quality even by raising the resolution. Polygonal models are also very basic. The panorama with its pixelated buildings in the distance and the 2D trees between one building and another is something technically of little relevance.
Technically it's nothing exceptional, you can't even move freely. You just have to wait for the scene to change until you get to the "flight" with the hot air balloon. In reality we will only rise about ten meters and then it will end. 

I don't understand why people praise it so much maybe because they have the chance to see the cathedral in 3D? Rather than seeing it so ugly with these poor polygonal models and these low quality textures it would be much better to see some videos at this point!

There are no graphic effects worth mentioning. This could very well run on quest 1
Maybe it would have caused a bit of a stir there, but we're now at quest 3!
Static viewpoints, no movement. Give ability to walk free inside!
Amazing and wonderful.  Brought me to tears.

Beautiful. Opulent. Breathtaking. Life changing. Sunday vibes for sure. This app should win an award. Better than a lot of paid apps that I bought. C'est fantastique. Formidable. Tres belle.

In the scenes where light enters the stained glass windows, the light cast on the wall is just a prosaic square of plain yellow sunlight. Boo! 5,000 hours? Looking at even photos of the specific light of the design of the stained glass windows cast on the wall on Sunday afternoon while listening to gentle 70s rock music is a magical experience. It is a timeless experience that takes one back to the magic of times decades past.
This app makes me want to get the paid app. If in this app, the light from the stained glass windows cast inside the Church from the sunlight outside is accurately depicted then my personal rating of this app would be 15 stars out of 10 rather than the 10 stars out of 10 as it stands now.

Deuteronomy 31:8
The Lord is the One who goes before you. He will never leave you nor forsake you. Do not worry nor be afraid.
Yes it's a little bit blurry but the world in there is so cool. You end up feeling like you're in a hot air balloon. Love!
Nice N.D. visit and impressive first VR experience without any motion sickness
